text: Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport (RED-S) affects both male and female athletes, not only females as previously conceptualized in the Female Athlete Triad.
RED-S is caused by energy deficiency relative to the balance between dietary energy intake and energy expenditure required for health, daily living, growth, and sporting activities.
RED-S impairs multiple physiological functions including metabolic rate, menstrual function, bone health, immunity, protein synthesis, and cardiovascular health.
Psychological consequences can both precede and result from RED-S, indicating a bidirectional relationship between mental health and energy deficiency.
Athletes with RED-S should be categorized into risk groups using a Sport Risk Assessment and Return to Play Model to guide clinical management and return-to-play decisions.
